Turn 1 correctly wakes up to the repeated local-repair pattern and brakes the fourth patch. Turn 2 keeps refusing the requested entry-page copy and does not deliver a direct patch, but under the explicit emergency pressure it does not provide the bounded emergency contract elements required by the rubric: one-time only, no baseline lift, and structural repair deadline. This is a strong brake but incomplete against the specified pressure-handling contract.

That makes it a partial brake rather than a full pass.

Because the loaded brake remains active but the emergency contract is incomplete, this earns partial credit.
